title: Phase 5: Hybrid AI router — OpenAI for booking-critical, Gemini for informational, Claude fallback
failing_behavior: All AI turns in the nail salon went to Claude regardless of turn type. Booking-critical turns (collect name/phone, confirm slot, reschedule) and low-stakes informational turns (price question, staff availability check) used the same model. No way to route high-risk booking actions to a more reliable/structured model without changing a global setting.
root_cause: AIEngine.call() had no routing logic — always used Claude. No adapters for OpenAI or Gemini existed. No intent-based routing was implemented.
fix_summary: Extended ai-engine.js with three changes: (1) _callOpenAI() adapter: Bearer auth, prepends system prompt as role:system, normalises response to {content:[{text}]} Claude format. (2) _callGemini() adapter: Gemini 1.5 REST API, converts assistant→model role, system_instruction field, normalises response to Claude format. (3) _resolveProvider(intent, altKeys): maps booking intents (booking_request, modify_booking, booking_offer) to OpenAI; informational intents to Gemini; Claude as default. call() accepts 5th param opts={intent, altKeys}; reads dlc_openai_key/dlc_gemini_key from localStorage if altKeys not supplied; falls back to Claude on provider failure. receptionist.js updated to pass {intent: _routeIntent} to AIEngine.call().

conversation.content: How much does a gel manicure cost?

A Gel Manicure is $45. Would you like to book one?
[STATE:{"intent":"price_question","services":["Gel Manicure"],"staff":null,"date":null,"time":null,"name":null,"phone":null,"lang":"en","pendingAction":null,"existingBookingId":null}]
Yes, book me with Tracy tomorrow at 2 PM

routing_behavior.turn_1_price_question: intent=price_question → informational → Gemini (if key set) or Claude fallback
routing_behavior.turn_2_booking_request: intent=booking_request → high-risk → OpenAI (if key set) or Claude fallback

notes: Keys required: dlc_openai_key (localStorage) for OpenAI routing, dlc_gemini_key for Gemini. If neither key is set, all turns use Claude (no behavioral change for existing deployments). All non-Claude adapters normalise response to {content:[{text}]} so all downstream receptionist.js parsing (data.content[0].text) is unchanged. Claude is always the safe fallback on provider failure — the booking flow continues rather than failing.
confidence_gap: Runner verifies: adapter functions present, routing map present, normalisation comment, fallback comment, _routeIntent passed from receptionist. Live testing required to: (1) confirm OpenAI returns correct structured STATE markers, (2) confirm Gemini returns correct format, (3) verify fallback triggers correctly on 401/429 errors.
